LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED $57,600 AS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS OF $3,195.45. THE COURT DID NOT APPORTION THE FEES FOR THE FRAUD AND CLRA WORK BECAUSE THE CLAIMS RAISE COMMON ISSUES THAT ARE NOT SEVERABLE. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR A MULTIPLIER. THE LODESTAR WAS $450.00/HR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077

REPLY DECLARATION OF MICHAEL R. QUIRK IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FOR FRAUD (TRANSACTION ID # 100022209) FILED BY PLAINTIFF RIVERA, MARIA LOPEZ NEGRETE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FOR FRAUD IS GRANTED IN PART (SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT). MS. RIVERA IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN HER FAVOR AND AGAINST THE FINEST JEWELRY.COM, INC., THE FINEST WATCHES.COM, INC. AND ROGER KUO, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, FOR BREACH OF THE PARTIES' OCTOBER 16, 2012 SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $44,542.06. MS. RIVERA IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT PER CCP 664.6 FOR BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE COURT IS NOT MAKING A DETERMINATION ONE WAY OR THE OTHER WHETHER THERE WAS OR WAS NOT FRAUD. WRITTEN ORDER AND JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Fraud
Off Calendar Jul-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Attorney, Michael R. Quirk (925-943-6400), for plaintiff/moving party, Maria Lopez Rivera
Attorney, Steven H. Bovarnick (415-957-1800), for defendants/opposing parties

The matter being argued and submitted, the Court modifies its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Fraud is granted in part. Ms. Rivera is entitled to judgment in her favor and against The Finest Jewelry.com, Inc., The Finest Watches.com, Inc. and Roger Kuo, jointly and severally, for breach of the parties' October 16, 2012 settlement agreement in the amount of $44,542.06. Ms. Rivera is entitled to judgment per CCP 664.6 for breach of the settlement agreement. The Court is not making a determination one way or the other whether there was or was not fraud. There are several ambiguities in the settlement agreement about how interest is to be calculated. The court made its own calculations based on what it believed to be the best and fairest interpretation of the agreement and, in doing so, credited each of the wire fee payments against the amounts owed. No attorney's fees are awarded to either side on this motion because neither side can fairly be described as the prevailing party. Ms. Rivera is not the prevailing party since her request for entry of a judgment for fraud has been rejected and the amount of the judgment is less than the amount she sought. Defendants are not the prevailing party since their request that the motion be denied in its entirety has been rejected. Written order and judgment are signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (D302)

Notice Of Motion For An Award Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael R. Quirk, Esq. of the Law Offices of Michael R. Quirk, T:925.943.6400, for plaintiff and moving party;
John A. Lofton, Esq. of Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick LLP, T:415.957.1800, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA's Motion For An Award Of Attorney Fees is granted. Plaintiff achieved her litigation objection in her Motion for Entry of Judgment and is thus considered the "prevailing party" pursuant to the parties' Settlement Agreement. Santisas v. Goodin (1998) 17 Cal.4th 599, 603-604 (holding the defendants were the "prevailing party" under the parties' real estate agreement when the plaintiffs voluntarily dismissed the action because the defendants' litigation objective was to prevent the plaintiffs from obtaining relief). Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $6,067.50, plus costs of $520.00. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR# 11185.

Notice Of Motion And Omtion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jan-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL R. QUIRK, TEL. NO. (925) 943-6400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEVEN H. BOVARNICK OF LPS LLP, TEL. NO. 957-1800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARIA RIVERA's Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs - The motion for fees and costs is granted. Plaintiff is awarded $57,600 as reasonable attorney's fees and costs of $3,195.45. The Court did not apportion the fees for the fraud and CLRA work because the claims raise common issues that are not severable. The Court denies the request for a multiplier. The lodestar was $450.00/hr. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/MJM)
